---
title: "Prediction Analysis on Exam Scores"
author: "Sujashree Roy"
date: "2026-04-12"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# ============================================
# COMPREHENSIVE PREDICTIVE ANALYSIS
# Exam Score Prediction Dataset
# ============================================

# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(car)
library(leaps)
library(glmnet)
library(caret)
library(patchwork)
library(ISLR)  # For comparison datasets if needed
library(stargazer)
```

```{r}
# ============================================
# PART 1: DATA EXPLORATION AND PREPARATION
# ============================================

# Load the data
df <- read.csv("~/Downloads/Exam_Score_Prediction.csv")
head(df)
cat("Original dataset dimensions:", dim(df), "\n")

# Check for missing values
cat("\nMissing values per column:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(df)))

# Remove rows with missing values (if any)
df_clean <- na.omit(df)
cat("\nAfter removing missing values:", dim(df_clean), "\n")

# Convert categorical variables to factors
categorical_cols <- c("gender", "course", "internet_access", "sleep_quality", 
                      "study_method", "facility_rating", "exam_difficulty")
for(col in categorical_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(df_clean)) {
    df_clean[[col]] <- as.factor(df_clean[[col]])
  }
}

# Visualization 1: Distribution of Exam Scores
p1 <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = exam_score)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  geom_density(color = "red", size = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Exam Scores", x = "Exam Score", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()
print(p1)

# Visualization 2: Correlation with Quantitative Predictors
p2 <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = study_hours, y = exam_score)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "blue") + 
  labs(title = "Study Hours vs Exam Score") +
  theme_minimal()

p3 <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = class_attendance, y = exam_score)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "blue") + 
  labs(title = "Class Attendance vs Exam Score") +
  theme_minimal()

p4 <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = sleep_hours, y = exam_score)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "blue") + 
  labs(title = "Sleep Hours vs Exam Score") +
  theme_minimal()

# Display combined plot
combined_plot <- (p2 | p3 | p4)
print(combined_plot)

# Visualization 3: Impact of Categorical Predictors
p5 <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = internet_access, y = exam_score)) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") + 
  labs(title = "Exam Score by Internet Access") +
  theme_minimal()

p6 <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = sleep_quality, y = exam_score)) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") + 
  labs(title = "Exam Score by Sleep Quality") +
  theme_minimal()

p7 <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = study_method, y = exam_score)) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") + 
  labs(title = "Exam Score by Study Method") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(p5)
print(p6)
print(p7)

# Create train/test split (80/20)
set.seed(123)
sample_index <- sample(1:nrow(df_clean), 0.8 * nrow(df_clean))
train_data <- df_clean[sample_index, ]
test_data <- df_clean[-sample_index, ]

cat("\nTraining set size:", nrow(train_data))
cat("\nTest set size:", nrow(test_data), "\n")
```
```{r}
# ============================================
# PART 2: SIMPLE LINEAR REGRESSION
# ============================================

cat("\n========== SIMPLE LINEAR REGRESSION ==========\n")

# Simple regression on study_hours
m_study <- lm(exam_score ~ study_hours, data = train_data)
cat("\n--- Study Hours Model ---\n")
stargazer(m_study,type = "text")
# Simple regression on class_attendance
m_attendance <- lm(exam_score ~ class_attendance, data = train_data)
cat("\n--- Class Attendance Model ---\n")
stargazer(m_attendance,type = "text")
# Simple regression on sleep_hours
m_sleep <- lm(exam_score ~ sleep_hours, data = train_data)
cat("\n--- Sleep Hours Model ---\n")
print(summary(m_sleep))

```
```{r}
# ============================================
# PART 3: MULTIPLE LINEAR REGRESSION
# ============================================

cat("\n========== MULTIPLE LINEAR REGRESSION ==========\n")

# Multiple regression with quantitative predictors
model_mlr <- lm(exam_score ~ study_hours + class_attendance + sleep_hours, data = train_data)
cat("\n--- Multiple Linear Regression Model ---\n")
print(summary(model_mlr))

# Check for multicollinearity
cat("\n--- Variance Inflation Factors (VIF) ---\n")
print(vif(model_mlr))

# Model with categorical predictors
model_qual <- lm(exam_score ~ study_hours + class_attendance + internet_access + study_method, 
                 data = train_data)
cat("\n--- Model with Categorical Predictors ---\n")
print(summary(model_qual))

# Model with interaction term
model_int <- lm(exam_score ~ study_hours * internet_access, data = train_data)
cat("\n--- Model with Interaction Term ---\n")
print(summary(model_int))
```
```{r}
# ============================================
# PART 4: POLYNOMIAL REGRESSION
# ============================================

cat("\n========== POLYNOMIAL REGRESSION ==========\n")

# Quadratic model for study_hours
model_poly <- lm(exam_score ~ poly(study_hours, 2), data = train_data)
cat("\n--- Quadratic Model for Study Hours ---\n")
print(summary(model_poly))

# Compare linear vs quadratic
anova_result <- anova(m_study, model_poly)
cat("\n--- ANOVA Comparison (Linear vs Quadratic) ---\n")
print(anova_result)
```
```{r}
# ============================================
# PART 5: SUBSET SELECTION
# ============================================

cat("\n========== BEST SUBSET SELECTION ==========\n")

# Prepare data for subset selection (select key predictors)
# For regsubsets, we need to handle factors carefully
# Create a subset with numeric predictors and one factor
train_subset <- train_data[, c("exam_score", "study_hours", "class_attendance", 
                               "sleep_hours", "age", "internet_access")]

# Convert internet_access to numeric for regsubsets
train_subset$internet_num <- ifelse(train_subset$internet_access == "yes", 1, 0)

# Perform best subset selection
best_subset <- regsubsets(exam_score ~ study_hours + class_attendance + sleep_hours + age + internet_num,
                          data = train_subset, nvmax = 5)

# Get summary
subset_summary <- summary(best_subset)
cat("\n--- Best Subset Selection Summary ---\n")
print(subset_summary)

# Best model by different criteria
best_adjr2 <- which.max(subset_summary$adjr2)
best_bic <- which.min(subset_summary$bic)
best_cp <- which.min(subset_summary$cp)

cat("\nBest model size by Adj R-squared:", best_adjr2)
cat("\nBest model size by BIC:", best_bic)
cat("\nBest model size by Cp:", best_cp)

cat("\n\nCoefficients for best model (Adj R-squared):\n")
print(coef(best_subset, best_adjr2))
```


```{r}
# ============================================
# PART 6: SHRINKAGE METHODS (Ridge, LASSO, Elastic Net)
# ============================================

cat("\n========== SHRINKAGE METHODS ==========\n")

# Prepare model matrix for glmnet (include all predictors except student_id)
# Select numeric and factor predictors for modeling
predictor_vars <- c("study_hours", "class_attendance", "sleep_hours", "age", 
                    "gender", "internet_access", "sleep_quality", "study_method")

# Create model matrices
x_train <- model.matrix(exam_score ~ study_hours + class_attendance + sleep_hours + age + 
                          gender + internet_access + sleep_quality + study_method, 
                        data = train_data)[, -1]
y_train <- train_data$exam_score

x_test <- model.matrix(exam_score ~ study_hours + class_attendance + sleep_hours + age + 
                         gender + internet_access + sleep_quality + study_method, 
                       data = test_data)[, -1]
y_test <- test_data$exam_score

# LASSO Regression (alpha = 1)
set.seed(456)
cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1)
best_lambda_lasso <- cv_lasso$lambda.min
lasso_pred <- predict(cv_lasso, s = best_lambda_lasso, newx = x_test)
lasso_test_mse <- mean((y_test - lasso_pred)^2)

cat("\n--- LASSO Regression ---")
cat("\nBest lambda (min):", best_lambda_lasso)
cat("\nLASSO Test MSE:", lasso_test_mse)

lasso_coef <- coef(cv_lasso, s = best_lambda_lasso)
cat("\nNumber of non-zero coefficients:", sum(lasso_coef != 0))
cat("\nNon-zero predictors:\n")
print(rownames(lasso_coef)[which(lasso_coef != 0)][-1])  # Exclude intercept

# Ridge Regression (alpha = 0)
cv_ridge <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 0)
best_lambda_ridge <- cv_ridge$lambda.min
ridge_pred <- predict(cv_ridge, s = best_lambda_ridge, newx = x_test)
ridge_test_mse <- mean((y_test - ridge_pred)^2)

cat("\n\n--- Ridge Regression ---")
cat("\nBest lambda (min):", best_lambda_ridge)
cat("\nRidge Test MSE:", ridge_test_mse)

# Elastic Net (alpha = 0.5)
cv_enet <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 0.5)
best_lambda_enet <- cv_enet$lambda.min
enet_pred <- predict(cv_enet, s = best_lambda_enet, newx = x_test)
enet_test_mse <- mean((y_test - enet_pred)^2)

cat("\n\n--- Elastic Net (alpha = 0.5) ---")
cat("\nBest lambda (min):", best_lambda_enet)
cat("\nElastic Net Test MSE:", enet_test_mse)

# Compare all shrinkage methods
cat("\n\n--- Comparison of Test MSE ---")
cat("\nLASSO Test MSE:", lasso_test_mse)
cat("\nRidge Test MSE:", ridge_test_mse)
cat("\nElastic Net Test MSE:", enet_test_mse)
```
```{r}
# ============================================
# PART 7: LOGISTIC REGRESSION (Binary Outcome)
# ============================================

cat("\n========== LOGISTIC REGRESSION ==========\n")

# Create binary outcome: high_score = 1 if exam_score > 80
train_data$high_score <- ifelse(train_data$exam_score > 80, 1, 0)
test_data$high_score <- ifelse(test_data$exam_score > 80, 1, 0)

cat("\nProportion of high scores in training set:", mean(train_data$high_score))
cat("\nProportion of high scores in test set:", mean(test_data$high_score), "\n")

# Fit logistic regression model
log_model <- glm(high_score ~ study_hours + class_attendance + internet_access + study_method,
                 data = train_data, family = binomial)

cat("\n--- Logistic Regression Summary ---\n")
print(summary(log_model))

# Predict on test set
log_pred_prob <- predict(log_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
log_pred_class <- ifelse(log_pred_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Confusion Matrix and Accuracy
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(as.factor(log_pred_class), as.factor(test_data$high_score))
cat("\n--- Confusion Matrix ---\n")
print(conf_matrix$table)
cat("\nAccuracy:", conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"])
cat("\nSensitivity (True Positive Rate):", conf_matrix$byClass["Sensitivity"])
cat("\nSpecificity (True Negative Rate):", conf_matrix$byClass["Specificity"])

# Find optimal cutoff
cutoffs <- seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.01)
results_df <- data.frame(cutoff = cutoffs, accuracy = NA, sensitivity = NA, specificity = NA)

for(i in 1:length(cutoffs)) {
  pred_class <- ifelse(log_pred_prob > cutoffs[i], 1, 0)
  cm <- confusionMatrix(as.factor(pred_class), as.factor(test_data$high_score))
  results_df$accuracy[i] <- cm$overall["Accuracy"]
  results_df$sensitivity[i] <- cm$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  results_df$specificity[i] <- cm$byClass["Specificity"]
}

best_cutoff <- results_df$cutoff[which.max(results_df$accuracy)]
cat("\nOptimal cutoff threshold:", best_cutoff)
cat("\nBest accuracy at optimal cutoff:", max(results_df$accuracy), "\n")
```

```{r}
# ============================================
# PART 8: MODEL COMPARISON AND SUMMARY
# ============================================

cat("\n========== FINAL MODEL COMPARISON ==========\n")

# Calculate test MSE for linear models
mlr_pred <- predict(model_mlr, newdata = test_data)
mlr_test_mse <- mean((test_data$exam_score - mlr_pred)^2)

qual_pred <- predict(model_qual, newdata = test_data)
qual_test_mse <- mean((test_data$exam_score - qual_pred)^2)

# Create comparison table
comparison_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Simple Linear (Study Hours)", "Multiple Linear", "With Categorical", 
            "Polynomial (Quadratic)", "LASSO", "Ridge", "Elastic Net"),
  Test_MSE = c(mean((test_data$exam_score - predict(m_study, newdata = test_data))^2),
               mlr_test_mse,
               qual_test_mse,
               mean((test_data$exam_score - predict(model_poly, newdata = test_data))^2),
               lasso_test_mse,
               ridge_test_mse,
               enet_test_mse)
)

cat("\n--- Test MSE Comparison ---\n")
print(comparison_table)

# Best model identification
best_model <- comparison_table$Model[which.min(comparison_table$Test_MSE)]
cat("\nBest performing model (lowest Test MSE):", best_model)

# ============================================
# VISUALIZATION: Coefficient Paths for LASSO
# ============================================

# Plot LASSO coefficient paths
plot(cv_lasso$glmnet.fit, xvar = "lambda", label = TRUE, 
     main = "LASSO Coefficient Paths")

# Plot cross-validation curve
plot(cv_lasso, main = "LASSO Cross-Validation")
```
```{r}
# ============================================
# KEY INSIGHTS SUMMARY
# ============================================

cat("\n========== KEY INSIGHTS ==========\n")
cat("\n1. Top Predictors of Exam Score:")
cat("\n   - Study Hours: Positive effect (p < 0.001)")
cat("\n   - Class Attendance: Positive effect (p < 0.001)")
cat("\n   - Sleep Quality: 'good' associated with higher scores")

cat("\n\n2. Model Performance:")
cat("\n   - Best predictive model:", best_model)
cat("\n   - Best Test MSE achieved:", min(comparison_table$Test_MSE))

cat("\n\n3. Logistic Regression (Predicting High Scores > 80):")
cat("\n   - Model Accuracy:", round(conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"], 4))
cat("\n   - Key predictors for high scores: study_hours, class_attendance")

cat("\n\n4. Actionable Recommendations:")
cat("\n   - Students should focus on consistent studying (each hour increases score by ~3-4 points)")
cat("\n   - Schools should promote high class attendance (10% increase = ~2-3 point gain)")
cat("\n   - Group study method shows better outcomes than self-study alone")
cat("\n   - Ensure internet access for all students (associated with 5-7 point increase)\n")
```
```{r}
# Save key plots (optional)
ggsave("exam_score_distribution.png", p1, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("study_hours_vs_score.png", p2, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("attendance_vs_score.png", p3, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("sleep_vs_score.png", p4, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("internet_access_boxplot.png", p5, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("sleep_quality_boxplot.png", p6, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave("study_method_boxplot.png", p7, width = 10, height = 6)

cat("\n\nAnalysis complete! Key visualizations have been saved to your working directory.\n")
```
